---
title: "S&P — CFD Futures"
---

S&P 500 exposure via CFD futures, sized with the same downside-volatility
targeting logic as every other Dog Capital program — adapted for a
CFD/futures account rather than spot ETFs.

<a href="../index.qmd#programs" class="back-link">← Back</a>

---

## Why CFD Futures

CFD futures give leveraged, capital-efficient exposure to the S&P 500 without
the capital lockup a fully-funded futures or equity position requires. For
Argentine investors specifically, this is also the direct-USD-exposure
counterpart to SPY Core's CEDEAR-based approach — a different venue and tax
treatment, sized and risk-managed with the same discipline.

Leverage without a systematic risk framework is how most CFD accounts fail.
The point of this program is the framework, not the leverage itself.

---

## Strategy Design

The position is sized against realized downside volatility, not a fixed
leverage multiple — the same core logic as [ES Core](es-core.qmd), adapted
for a single-instrument CFD/futures account instead of a multi-asset
portfolio.

**Framework:**

- Single instrument: S&P 500
- Volatility target: 20% annualized downside semi-deviation
- Leverage: dynamic, bounded 0–3.0x
- EMA(10) smoothing on the leverage signal
- 21-day realized volatility lookback
- Equity-based position sizing — exposure scales with account equity, not a
  fixed lot size
- Automated drawdown and rollover alerting

Exposure contracts automatically as realized volatility rises and expands as
it falls — no discretionary override, no directional forecasting.

---

## Track Record

This program has a real, live discretionary track record that predates the
systematic version above — manually traded, run over more than a year before
any automation existed. The systematic strategy is currently proving itself
on a demo account before taking over live execution.

Once the systematic track record has enough live history to stand on its
own, this page will carry a real performance comparison — discretionary vs.
systematic vs. a passive S&P 500 benchmark — the same standard every other
Dog Capital program is held to. No hypothetical or backtested figures are
shown here in the meantime.

---

## Execution Layer

Signal generation and position sizing run on a dedicated automated terminal,
independent of the ETF/CEDEAR and IBKR-futures execution used by the other
programs. Risk alerting (drawdown, rollover) runs alongside the sizing logic,
not as a separate manual check.

---

*© 2026 Dog Capital*
